# Redstone Lake Cottagers Association

> Volunteer-run, not-for-profit cottagers' association (founded 1961) protecting
> seven lakes in the Redstone River watershed, Haliburton County, Ontario,
> Canada: Redstone, Little Redstone, Pelaw, Bitter, Long (Tedious), Burdock and
> Coleman Lakes. At the July 2026 AGM members approved renaming the association
> to the Redstone Area Lakes Association (RALA); the change takes effect once
> corporate filings complete.

Key facts:
- Membership: $30/1yr, $60/2yr, $80/3yr; open to property owners within the
  Redstone group of lakes. Contact membership@redstonelake.com
- Annual General Meeting: second Saturday of July, Haliburton Forest
- The site shows live Redstone Lake water levels (Parks Canada), local weather,
  and the Municipality of Dysart et al fire-ban status
- Open-air burning in fire season (Apr 1–Oct 31) is permitted only 7 p.m.–7 a.m.
  under the county by-law; always verify current fire bans with the municipality

## Main pages
- [Home](/): live water level, weather, fire-ban status, events and news
- [News & Articles](/news): community news archive since 2021
- [Lake Health Data](/lake-health): ~30 years of provincial water quality data
  (phosphorus, clarity, calcium, chloride, sulphate) for all seven lakes
- [Membership](/membership): dues, benefits, how to join
- [Newsletters](/newsletters): monthly newsletter archive since 2020
- [Annual General Meeting](/agm): minutes and presentations, 2020–2026
- [Governing Documents](/governance): letters patent and by-laws since 1961
- [Board Members](/board-members): current volunteer board
- [Sponsors & Business Directory](/business-directory): local businesses
- [New Cottager Guide](/new-cottager-guide), [Healthy Shoreline](/healthy-shoreline),
  [Septic Systems](/septic-systems), [Get the Lead Out](/get-the-lead-out)
- [Contact](/contact): communications@redstonelake.com

## Policies
- [Privacy Policy](/privacy-policy)
- [Terms of Use](/terms) — information is provided as-is by volunteers; verify
  safety-critical information (fire bans, water conditions) with authorities
